pred_reason: SWC-107 is appropriate because the approveAndCall function violates the Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern by performing state changes before external calls. This creates a classic reentrancy vulnerability where malicious contracts can reenter and manipulate state. Evidence: External call to spender occurs after state changes in approveAndCall; No reentrancy guards or CEI pattern enforcement detected; Line 130 directly calls external contract without security checks

findings.Description: The approveAndCall function violates Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern by making external calls after state changes, enabling reentrancy attacks. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Malicious contracts could re-enter and manipulate contract state, potentially leading to fund theft
findings.Location: approveAndCall function, line 130 (external call to spender)

findings.Description: The approveAndCall function does not check return values from external calls to spender contracts. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: External call failures may go undetected, leading to inconsistent state and failed integrations

findings.Description: Contract uses Solidity 0.4.24 which lacks modern security features and contains known vulnerabilities. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Increased attack surface, missing built-in safety features, compatibility issues with modern tooling
